8	# CONTRACT-004: Harness Implementation Contract
9	
10	| Field | Value |
11	|-------|-------|
12	| Status | Draft |
13	| Date | 2026-05-14 |
14	| Scope | Internal interface every Fizeau harness package implements; service-side consumers depend only on this contract, never on harness-specific exports. |
15	| Companion | CONTRACT-003 (service-facing API); ADR-014 (decision rationale) |
16	
17	## Purpose
18	
19	CONTRACT-003 specifies the public Fizeau service surface. It does not specify
20	the internal contract that each harness implementation
21	(`internal/harnesses/<name>/`) satisfies. Today that internal contract is the
22	3-method `harnesses.Harness` interface plus an ad-hoc collection of
23	per-harness exported symbols (~80 of them across claude, codex, gemini)
24	that the service imports directly: `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot`,
25	`ReadClaudeQuota`, `DecideClaudeQuotaRouting`, `CodexAuthPath`,
26	`ReadAuthEvidence`, `ResolveClaudeFamilyAlias`, and so on.
27	
28	This contract closes that gap. It defines the complete normative interface
29	every harness implementation MUST satisfy and constrains the service to
30	consume only that interface. Per-harness symbol leakage is the failure
31	mode CONTRACT-004 exists to prevent.
32	
33	## Scope
34	
35	In scope:
36	
37	- The Go interface set every `internal/harnesses/<name>/` package
38	  implements.
39	- The universal types those interfaces return.
40	- Cache and refresh ownership.
41	- Conformance evidence requirements.
42	- Projection rules from internal harness types to the CONTRACT-003
43	  public types (`QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`, `HarnessInfo`,
44	  `ProviderInfo`).
45	
46	Out of scope:
47	
48	- The CLI/network behavior of any specific harness binary (covered by
49	  primary-harness-capability-baseline.md and per-harness specs).
50	- The cassette/replay transport contract (ADR-002).
51	- The public service contract (CONTRACT-003).
52	
53	## Interface Set
54	
55	Every harness implementation MUST implement `Harness`. It MAY additionally
56	implement any of `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, and
57	`ModelDiscoveryHarness`. The service uses Go interface assertions to
58	discover which optional contracts a harness satisfies and consumes them
59	through the interface only.
60	
61	```go
62	package harnesses
63	
64	// Harness is the minimum every harness implements.
65	type Harness interface {
66	    // Info returns identity + capability metadata. Stable across the
67	    // process lifetime; cheap to call from request hot paths.
68	    Info() HarnessInfo
69	
70	    // HealthCheck triggers a fresh, lightweight readiness probe (binary
71	    // present and executable; non-blocking auth state read where
72	    // possible). Returns nil when ready. MUST NOT drive interactive
73	    // sessions or block on network.
74	    HealthCheck(ctx context.Context) error
75	
76	    // Execute runs one resolved request and returns an event channel.
77	    // The channel emits zero or more progress/text/tool events followed
78	    // by exactly one EventTypeFinal event, then closes. Setup failures
79	    // (binary missing, PTY allocation failure) return as the second
80	    // return value; per-run failures (auth, quota, parser desync,
81	    // timeout, cancellation) MUST be reported via the final event with
82	    // Status != "success".
83	    Execute(ctx context.Context, req ExecuteRequest) (<-chan Event, error)
84	}
85	
86	// QuotaHarness is implemented by harnesses that own a subscription or
87	// quota window. claude, codex, gemini implement; opencode, pi do not.
88	type QuotaHarness interface {
89	    Harness
90	
91	    // QuotaStatus returns the current quota state from the harness's
92	    // owned cache, with Fresh/Age computed against `now`. MUST be cheap
93	    // (no live probe) and safe to call on every routing decision.
94	    // Absence of evidence is reported via State=QuotaUnavailable on a
95	    // valid QuotaStatus value; the error return is reserved for call
96	    // failure (ctx cancelled, IO failure, lock acquisition failure).
97	    QuotaStatus(ctx context.Context, now time.Time) (QuotaStatus, error)
98	
99	    // RefreshQuota drives the harness's live probe (PTY, CLI, or other
100	    // owned transport), persists the result through the harness's owned
101	    // cache, and returns the resulting status. RefreshQuota probes
102	    // unconditionally on every successful lock acquisition; it does
103	    // NOT skip the probe based on cache freshness. Cache-freshness
104	    // deduplication is the caller's responsibility: callers that want
105	    // a possibly-stale-but-cheap read use QuotaStatus; callers that
106	    // orchestrate refresh cadence (e.g. a periodic scheduler) skip
107	    // ticks when the last-known CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness()
108	    // of the current time. Callers that want a fresh probe call
109	    // RefreshQuota and accept the probe cost.
110	    //
111	    // RefreshQuota is single-flight per harness instance: concurrent
112	    // callers block on the harness's cache lock (per ADR-012). Queued
113	    // callers MUST NOT initiate a second probe — once the in-flight
114	    // refresh completes and releases the lock, queued callers read the
115	    // just-written cached state and return it. The contract guarantee
116	    // is "one probe per single-flight cohort," not "one probe per
117	    // call." Probe failure is reported as a QuotaStatus with
118	    // State=QuotaUnavailable (or QuotaUnauthenticated when the probe
119	    // identifies the failure as auth-related), not as an error. The
120	    // error return is reserved for call failure: ctx cancelled before
121	    // lock acquisition, lock acquisition timeout under deadline,
122	    // unrecoverable lock-file corruption, or unrecoverable IO failure.
123	    // Lock contention itself is not a failure — the caller waits.
124	    RefreshQuota(ctx context.Context) (QuotaStatus, error)
125	
126	    // QuotaFreshness returns the harness's freshness window (e.g. 15m).
127	    // Service code uses this for stale-cache scheduling. Constant for
128	    // the harness; cheap to call.
129	    QuotaFreshness() time.Duration
130	
131	    // SupportedLimitIDs returns the harness's stable set of emitted
132	    // Windows[].LimitID values (e.g. "session", "weekly-all",
133	    // "weekly-sonnet" for claude; "tier-flash", "tier-pro" for
134	    // gemini). Constant for the harness; the harness's package doc
135	    // also enumerates this set for human readers. The conformance
136	    // suite reads this value to verify that emitted Windows[].LimitID
137	    // strings are a subset of the documented set, and that the set
138	    // does not regress between binary versions without a deprecation
139	    // cycle. Empty slice is allowed for harnesses that emit no
140	    // windows.
141	    SupportedLimitIDs() []string
142	}
143	
144	// AccountHarness is implemented by harnesses that expose authentication
145	// or account state independent of quota. gemini implements this so its
146	// AuthSnapshot can refresh on its own cadence (7-day freshness window vs.
147	// quota's 15-minute window). claude and codex embed account in their
148	// quota probe and MAY satisfy AccountHarness by re-projecting from
149	// QuotaStatus.Account; that is allowed but not required.
150	type AccountHarness interface {
151	    Harness
152	
153	    // AccountStatus returns the harness's current account/auth state.
154	    // Cheap; reads cached evidence only. Absence of evidence is
155	    // reported via AccountSnapshot.Authenticated=false and
156	    // .Unauthenticated=false (i.e. unknown) on a valid snapshot; the
157	    // error return is reserved for call failure.
158	    AccountStatus(ctx context.Context, now time.Time) (AccountSnapshot, error)
159	
160	    // RefreshAccount drives the harness's account probe (file read,
161	    // CLI call, OAuth state lookup) and persists the result.
162	    // Single-flight per harness instance via the harness's account
163	    // cache lock; concurrent callers block. Probe failure is reported
164	    // via AccountSnapshot fields, not as an error.
165	    RefreshAccount(ctx context.Context) (AccountSnapshot, error)
166	
167	    // AccountFreshness returns the harness's account freshness window
168	    // (e.g. 7 days for gemini, 15 minutes for harnesses whose account
169	    // refreshes coupled with quota). Constant for the harness; cheap.
170	    // Used by the service refresh scheduler to decide when account
171	    // state is stale independent of quota state.
172	    AccountFreshness() time.Duration
173	}
174	
175	// ModelDiscoveryHarness is implemented by harnesses whose model surface
176	// extends beyond a single Info().DefaultModel — i.e. they support family
177	// aliases (sonnet, gpt, gemini) that resolve through discovery evidence.
178	// All five harnesses implement this in the current registry.
179	type ModelDiscoveryHarness interface {
180	    Harness
181	
182	    // DefaultModelSnapshot returns the harness's seed/fallback discovery
183	    // snapshot. Used to bootstrap the catalog before the first live
184	    // refresh lands. Stable for the harness; cheap.
185	    DefaultModelSnapshot() ModelDiscoverySnapshot
186	
187	    // ResolveModelAlias maps a family-style requested model (e.g.
188	    // "sonnet", "gpt", "gemini") to a concrete model ID using the
189	    // provided discovery snapshot. Returns ErrAliasNotResolvable if the
190	    // family is not recognized or the snapshot has no matching concrete
191	    // model.
192	    ResolveModelAlias(family string, snapshot ModelDiscoverySnapshot) (string, error)
193	
194	    // SupportedAliases returns the harness's stable set of family
195	    // aliases ResolveModelAlias recognizes (e.g. "sonnet", "opus",
196	    // "haiku" for claude). Constant for the harness; the harness's
197	    // package doc also enumerates this set for human readers. The
198	    // conformance suite uses this value to verify ResolveModelAlias
199	    // covers each documented family (positive path) and rejects
200	    // out-of-set families with ErrAliasNotResolvable (negative path).
201	    // Empty slice is allowed for harnesses that recognize no family
202	    // aliases (e.g. opencode, pi).
203	    SupportedAliases() []string
204	}
205	```
206	
207	## Universal Types
208	
209	```go
210	package harnesses
211	
212	// QuotaStatus is the universal report consumed by service-side routing,
213	// status assembly, and operator surfaces. Each harness's private snapshot
214	// type projects into this; the private snapshot is never exposed.
215	type QuotaStatus struct {
216	    // Source identifies how the underlying evidence was captured:
217	    // "pty", "cache", "session-token-count", "cli", "api".
218	    Source string
219	
220	    // CapturedAt is when the underlying evidence was observed (not when
221	    // this status struct was assembled).
222	    CapturedAt time.Time
223	
224	    // Fresh reports whether CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() at the
225	    // time of the call.
226	    Fresh bool
227	
228	    // Age is now - CapturedAt at the time of the call.
229	    Age time.Duration
230	
231	    // State is the normalized state. Only QuotaOK and QuotaStale carry
232	    // routing-usable signal; others MUST NOT result in
233	    // RoutingPreferenceAvailable.
234	    State QuotaStateValue
235	
236	    // Windows captures per-window evidence (5h, weekly, tier-specific).
237	    // Authoritative for any structured fact the routing layer or
238	    // operator surfaces consume — including tier breakdowns. Each
239	    // window's LimitID distinguishes it (e.g. "session", "weekly-all",
240	    // "weekly-sonnet", "tier-flash", "tier-pro"). Empty windows are
241	    // allowed for harnesses that report aggregate state only.
242	    //
243	    // LimitID values are part of the harness's stable public contract:
244	    // once a harness ships a LimitID, the routing layer may depend on
245	    // it and the harness MUST NOT silently rename, remove, or repurpose
246	    // it. Adding new LimitIDs is additive and safe. Renames go through
247	    // a deprecation cycle with both old and new IDs present long
248	    // enough for downstream consumers to migrate. Each harness's
249	    // package documentation enumerates its emitted LimitID set; the
250	    // primary-harness-capability-baseline tracks the canonical list.
251	    Windows []QuotaWindow
252	
253	    // Account is the account/plan/auth evidence captured alongside
254	    // quota. Nil when the harness has no concept of account or when
255	    // account evidence is delivered through AccountHarness only.
256	    Account *AccountSnapshot
257	
258	    // RoutingPreference indicates whether the routing layer should
259	    // prefer this harness given the current evidence. Encapsulates the
260	    // PreferClaude/PreferCodex/PreferGemini distinctions today.
261	    RoutingPreference RoutingPreference
262	
263	    // Reason is a short human-readable explanation of State and
264	    // RoutingPreference — surfaced in operator views and routing logs.
265	    Reason string
266	
267	    // Detail is harness-specific opaque metadata for diagnostic display
268	    // only. Service code MAY surface it verbatim in operator views;
269	    // service code MUST NOT branch on its keys or values for routing
270	    // decisions. Detail MUST NOT carry structured facts that the
271	    // routing layer needs — those belong in Windows. Detail is for
272	    // free-form notes (e.g. "captured at boot, before first refresh"),
273	    // not tier breakdowns or window data.
274	    Detail map[string]string
275	}
276	
277	// QuotaStateValue is the normalized state enumeration.
278	type QuotaStateValue string
279	
280	const (
281	    QuotaOK              QuotaStateValue = "ok"
282	    QuotaStale           QuotaStateValue = "stale"
283	    QuotaBlocked         QuotaStateValue = "blocked"
284	    QuotaUnavailable     QuotaStateValue = "unavailable"
285	    QuotaUnauthenticated QuotaStateValue = "unauthenticated"
286	    QuotaUnknown         QuotaStateValue = "unknown"
287	)
288	
289	// RoutingPreference is the routing layer's consumable signal.
290	type RoutingPreference int
291	
292	const (
293	    RoutingPreferenceUnknown   RoutingPreference = iota
294	    RoutingPreferenceAvailable
295	    RoutingPreferenceBlocked
296	)
297	
298	// AccountSnapshot is the universal account/auth report. Projects onto
299	// the public AccountStatus type defined in CONTRACT-003.
300	type AccountSnapshot struct {
301	    Authenticated   bool
302	    Unauthenticated bool
303	    Email           string
304	    PlanType        string
305	    OrgName         string
306	    Source          string         // file path, env var name, "cache", "cli"
307	    CapturedAt      time.Time
308	    Fresh           bool
309	    Detail          string         // free-form diagnostic detail
310	}
311	
312	// Sentinel errors for interface methods. Note: absence of quota or
313	// account evidence is NOT an error — it is reported via State or
314	// Authenticated/Unauthenticated fields on a valid returned value.
315	// Errors are reserved for call failure.
316	var (
317	    ErrAliasNotResolvable = errors.New("model alias not resolvable from snapshot")
318	)
319	```
320	
321	`QuotaWindow`, `ModelDiscoverySnapshot`, `Event`, `ExecuteRequest`,
322	`HarnessInfo`, and `EventType` retain their existing definitions in
323	`internal/harnesses/types.go`. CONTRACT-004 does not redefine them.
324	
325	## Cache and Refresh Ownership
326	
327	Each `QuotaHarness` and `AccountHarness` implementation owns:
328	
329	- The on-disk cache path (defaulted to `$XDG_STATE_HOME/<harness>/quota.json`
330	  or `$XDG_STATE_HOME/<harness>/account.json`; harness-specific paths are
331	  acceptable when documented).
332	- The cache schema, including version bumps and migration.
333	- The freshness window returned by `QuotaFreshness()`.
334	- The probe transport (PTY, CLI, file read).
335	- Lock coordination per ADR-012 (per-source on-disk cache).
336	
337	Service-side code MUST NOT read, write, or compute paths for these caches
338	directly. Operator-visible cache paths SHOULD be exposed through a single
339	service-level diagnostic surface (e.g. `Service.DiagnosticPaths()`) that
340	calls back into each harness via a documented method, rather than the
341	service importing per-harness path functions.
342	
343	## Projection to CONTRACT-003
344	
345	The service layer projects harness-level types onto CONTRACT-003 public
346	types as follows. The projection is the only place service code converts
347	between layers; nothing else in the service may read fields off
348	`QuotaStatus` and re-emit them under different names.
349	
350	| CONTRACT-003 public field | CONTRACT-004 source |
351	|---------------------------|---------------------|
352	| `service.QuotaState.Windows` | `QuotaStatus.Windows` |
353	| `service.QuotaState.CapturedAt` | `QuotaStatus.CapturedAt` |
354	| `service.QuotaState.Fresh` | `QuotaStatus.Fresh` |
355	| `service.QuotaState.Source` | `QuotaStatus.Source` |
356	| `service.QuotaState.Status` | `QuotaStatus.State` (string-cast); when `QuotaStatus.Reason` is non-empty the projection appends ` (<reason>)` so operator surfaces preserve the explanation regardless of `State` value |
357	| `service.QuotaState.LastError` | Non-nil only when the harness method itself returned an error (call failure). State-driven absence (`State=QuotaUnavailable` etc.) does not populate `LastError`; it populates `Status`. |
358	| `service.AccountStatus.Authenticated` | `AccountSnapshot.Authenticated` |
359	| `service.AccountStatus.Unauthenticated` | `AccountSnapshot.Unauthenticated` |
360	| `service.AccountStatus.Email` | `AccountSnapshot.Email` |
361	| `service.AccountStatus.PlanType` | `AccountSnapshot.PlanType` |
362	| `service.AccountStatus.OrgName` | `AccountSnapshot.OrgName` |
363	| `service.AccountStatus.Source` | `AccountSnapshot.Source` |
364	| `service.AccountStatus.CapturedAt` | `AccountSnapshot.CapturedAt` |
365	| `service.AccountStatus.Fresh` | `AccountSnapshot.Fresh` |
366	| `service.AccountStatus.Detail` | `AccountSnapshot.Detail` |
367	| `service.HarnessInfo.Quota` | Result of `QuotaHarness.QuotaStatus()` projected via the rows above; nil when the harness does not implement `QuotaHarness` |
368	| `service.HarnessInfo.Account` | Result of `AccountHarness.AccountStatus()` projected; nil when the harness does not implement `AccountHarness` |
369	| `service.ProviderInfo.Quota` | Same projection as `HarnessInfo.Quota` for subscription-backed providers |
370	| `service.ProviderInfo.Auth` | Same projection as `HarnessInfo.Account` |
371	
372	Routing decisions inside the service consume `QuotaStatus.RoutingPreference`
373	directly. The service MUST NOT project `RoutingPreference` into the public
374	contract; it remains an internal routing signal.
375	
376	`HarnessInfo.Quota` and `HarnessInfo.Account` projections MUST remain
377	backwards-compatible in JSON shape with the current CONTRACT-003 schema.
378	Adding fields is allowed; removing or renaming existing public fields
379	requires a CONTRACT-003 amendment.
380	
381	## Conformance Evidence
382	
383	Every harness implementation MUST carry, at minimum:
384	
385	| Capability | Evidence |
386	|------------|----------|
387	| `Harness` (all) | Unit tests for `Info()`, `HealthCheck()`, and an `Execute` happy path with a final event of correct shape. |
388	| `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex, gemini) | Unit tests for `QuotaStatus` against a synthetic cache fixture (fresh and stale cases); unit tests for `RefreshQuota` against a recorded cassette per ADR-002; unit test asserting `QuotaFreshness` is the documented constant; conformance assertion that every emitted `Windows[].LimitID` value is a member of `SupportedLimitIDs()`. |
389	| `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex) | Unit tests for `AccountStatus` returning each documented state (Authenticated, Unauthenticated, no evidence) with correct `Fresh` against a synthetic file fixture. |
390	| `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five) | Unit tests for `DefaultModelSnapshot` returning a non-empty model list. Conformance assertion that `ResolveModelAlias` resolves every family returned by `SupportedAliases()` (positive path) and returns `ErrAliasNotResolvable` for an out-of-set family (negative path). The package documentation MUST enumerate the same set returned by `SupportedAliases()` for human readers; the conformance check is programmatic against `SupportedAliases()`. Stability rule: additive aliases are safe; removing or renaming an alias goes through a deprecation cycle (same as `Windows[].LimitID`). |
391	| Projection | Service-level test asserting CONTRACT-003 JSON shape (e.g. `HarnessInfo.Quota`) is identical before and after the harness migration — pinned to a recorded fixture. |
392	
393	## Invariants
394	
395	These invariants are enforced by reviewer attention and (where practical)
396	by `go vet`-shaped tooling:
397	
398	1. **No service-side import of `internal/harnesses/<name>` symbols beyond
399	   `Runner{}` construction.** Permitted imports from outside the harness's
400	   own package and `internal/harnesses` itself: the runner constructor
401	   used by `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`. All other
402	   consumption MUST go through the interface methods on `Harness`,
403	   `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, or `ModelDiscoveryHarness`.
404	2. **Cache I/O is harness-owned.** No code outside
405	   `internal/harnesses/<name>/` reads or writes the harness's cache
406	   files.
407	3. **Concrete snapshot types are package-private.** The per-harness
408	   types that today are exported as `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot`,
409	   `CodexQuotaSnapshot`, `GeminiQuotaSnapshot`, etc., become lowercase
410	   (package-private). The interface contract returns `QuotaStatus`.
411	4. **Service routing reads only `RoutingPreference`, `State`, and
412	   `Windows`.** No service-side scoring rule may branch on `Detail`
413	   map contents or internal fields of the per-harness snapshot.
414	   `Windows` is the authoritative structured surface for tier or
415	   per-window facts.
416	5. **No cross-harness imports.** `claude` does not import `codex`;
417	   `claude-tui` (when introduced) does not import `claude`; etc. Shared
418	   helpers go in a neutral package (e.g.
419	   `internal/harnesses/anthropic/`).
420	6. **Runners are stateless wrappers around harness-owned cache files.**
421	   The `Runner` struct MAY hold immutable configuration (binary path,
422	   discovery cache pointer) and lock handles; it MUST NOT hold mutable
423	   quota or account state that would diverge across instances. Two
424	   `&Runner{}` instances of the same harness MUST observe identical
425	   `QuotaStatus`/`AccountSnapshot` results for the same cache state.
426	   The service MAY hold a single registered instance per harness or
427	   construct fresh instances per call; both patterns MUST produce
428	   equivalent observable behavior.
429	7. **`RefreshQuota` and `RefreshAccount` are single-flight per harness
430	   instance, mediated by the harness's on-disk cache lock (ADR-012).**
431	   Concurrent callers block on the lock; once the in-flight refresh
432	   completes, queued callers observe the new cached state. Probe
433	   failure surfaces in the returned status's `State` field, not as an
434	   error return.
435	8. **Errors are reserved for call failure.** `QuotaStatus`,
436	   `RefreshQuota`, `AccountStatus`, and `RefreshAccount` return an
437	   error ONLY for context cancellation, lock acquisition failure, or
438	   unrecoverable IO error. Absence of evidence, missing auth, blocked
439	   quota, and probe failures are reported as state on a valid
440	   returned value. This makes consumer code uniform across success and
441	   failure surfaces.
442	
443	## Non-Goals
444	
445	- Refactoring CONTRACT-003 public types. Their shapes stay the same;
446	  only their *source* changes from per-harness exports to interface
447	  projection.
448	- Generalizing the `HarnessConfig` registry struct. Subprocess-specific
449	  fields (`BaseArgs`, `PermissionArgs`, `ModelFlag`) remain on the
450	  registry struct; they are configuration, not part of the interface.
451	- Introducing a plugin loader or out-of-tree harness support. Harnesses
452	  remain in-tree under `internal/harnesses/`.
453	- Promoting `claude-tui` to a primary harness identity. ADR-013 is
454	  withdrawn pending this contract; re-proposal is a follow-up spec.
455	
456	## Acceptance Criteria
457	
458	1. `internal/harnesses/types.go` declares `Harness`, `QuotaHarness`,
459	   `AccountHarness`, and `ModelDiscoveryHarness` interfaces with the
460	   signatures above.
461	2. `QuotaStatus`, `AccountSnapshot`, `QuotaStateValue`,
462	   `RoutingPreference`, and the three sentinel errors are declared in
463	   `internal/harnesses/types.go`.
464	3. Every existing harness implementation in `internal/harnesses/<name>/`
465	   satisfies `Harness` plus the documented optional sub-interfaces.
466	4. No `.go` file outside `internal/harnesses/` imports a symbol from
467	   `internal/harnesses/claude`, `internal/harnesses/codex`,
468	   `internal/harnesses/gemini`, `internal/harnesses/opencode`, or
469	   `internal/harnesses/pi` other than the runner constructor used by
470	   `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`. A linter check enforces
471	   this.
472	5. Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes for `HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
473	   `QuotaState`, and `AccountStatus` are byte-identical (modulo
474	   intentionally added fields) to pre-refactor fixtures.
475	6. Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot` types are lowercased (package-private).
476	7. Per-harness `*QuotaRoutingDecision` types are removed in favor of
477	   `QuotaStatus.RoutingPreference`.
478	8. Per-harness cache I/O functions (`Read*Quota`, `Write*Quota`,
479	   `*QuotaCachePath`) are unexported.
480	9. Conformance evidence (above) lands for every existing harness.
481	10. CONTRACT-003 cross-references CONTRACT-004 as the source for
